pages.raw_text:
FURNISHED by Walter a. Nichols &


Company


NEW YORK, July 14-According
to the understanding of the railroad
officials here, the decision reached by
the Interstate Commerce Commission
to suspend The operation of higher
freight rates in this particular case
does not apply to the advances which
were to be issued by the western
roads. From the information receiV-
ed from Washington it appears that
such roads as the St Paul, Northern
Pacific, Great Northern, Chicago q
Northwestern, Union Pacific South
ern Pacific, Atchison and Denver &
Rio Grande will not be affected by
this particular order. The decision
however, will affect all the eastern
trunk lines and the roads embraced
in the central traffic association. The
roads. it is said, filed their increased
rates with the understanding that
they would be held up pending inves-
tigation by the committee in the case
of western roads. However, no such
understanding existed. All of the
trunk line and railroads between the
Atlantic coast and the Missouri river
are concerned in the suspension of
freight rates by the Interstate Com
merce Commission today.
